note: Reverses the fix for the LiteLLM Proxy API-key-verification SQL injection (GHSA-r75f-5x8p-qvmc, Critical CVSS 9.3 / CWE-89), the AI gateway most production stacks put in front of their models. PrismaClient's combined_view token lookup built its SQL with the caller-supplied token mixed into the query text (WHERE v.token = '{token}') instead of binding it as a parameter; an unauthenticated request with a crafted Authorization: Bearer header reaches this query through the proxy's error-handling path, allowing read/modify of the proxy's own database (keys, budgets, spend). The fix bound the value as a parameter (WHERE v.token = $1, passed separately) and dropped the f-string. This suite reverses that: it restores the f-string interpolation of the raw token into the query and removes the bound parameter. The disguise: the value flows from an auth header through a key-verification helper, not an obvious user input, and a sibling variable named hashed_token sits right there, so a reviewer has to notice that raw token (not the hash) is concatenated into SQL.

bugs.severity_note: GHSA-r75f-5x8p-qvmc is rated Critical (CVSS 9.3): unauthenticated SQL injection in the proxy key-verification path, reachable via a crafted Authorization header, allowing read/modify of the proxy database. Mapped to the board's high (its top tier), not inflated.
bugs.description: Reverts the fix for the LiteLLM Proxy key-verification SQL injection (GHSA-r75f-5x8p-qvmc). In PrismaClient's combined_view branch, the token lookup builds a raw SQL string and runs it via _query_first_with_cached_plan_fallback. The fixed code binds the token as a parameter: the query reads WHERE v.token = $1 and hashed_token is passed as a separate argument (a plain, non-f string). This regression restores the vulnerable form: the string becomes an f-string again and interpolates the raw, caller-supplied token directly into the query text (WHERE v.token = '{token}'), and the bound parameter is removed. Because LiteLLM Proxy reaches this lookup during API-key verification on every LLM route, an unauthenticated attacker who sends a crafted Authorization: Bearer value can break out of the quoted literal and inject arbitrary SQL (reachable through the proxy's error-handling path per the advisory), reading or modifying the proxy's own database, the keys, budgets and spend it manages. The catch: a reviewer must see that the raw token (not the adjacent hashed_token) is concatenated into a SQL string via an f-string instead of being passed as a bound parameter, i.e. classic SQL injection. Flagging a generic 'string formatting in SQL' is partial; the catch names SQL injection and that the parameter binding was removed.

injection.find:
sql_query = """
                        SELECT 
                            v.*,
                            t.spend AS team_spend, 
                            t.max_budget AS team_max_budget,
                            t.soft_budget AS team_soft_budget,
                            t.tpm_limit AS team_tpm_limit,
                            t.rpm_limit AS team_rpm_limit,
                            t.models AS team_models,
                            t.metadata AS team_metadata,
                            t.blocked AS team_blocked,
                            t.team_alias AS team_alias,
                            t.metadata AS team_metadata,
                            t.members_with_roles AS team_members_with_roles,
                            t.object_permission_id AS team_object_permission_id,
                            t.organization_id as org_id,
                            p.project_alias AS project_alias,
                            tm.spend AS team_member_spend,
                            b_tm.tpm_limit AS team_member_tpm_limit,
                            b_tm.rpm_limit AS team_member_rpm_limit,
                            m.aliases AS team_model_aliases,
                            -- Added comma to separate b.* columns
                            b.max_budget AS litellm_budget_table_max_budget,
                            b.tpm_limit AS litellm_budget_table_tpm_limit,
                            b.rpm_limit AS litellm_budget_table_rpm_limit,
                            b.model_max_budget as litellm_budget_table_model_max_budget,
                            b.soft_budget as litellm_budget_table_soft_budget,
                            o.metadata as organization_metadata,
                            o.organization_alias as organization_alias,
                            b2.max_budget as organization_max_budget,
                            b2.tpm_limit as organization_tpm_limit,
                            b2.rpm_limit as organization_rpm_limit
                        FROM "LiteLLM_VerificationToken" AS v
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_TeamTable" AS t ON v.team_id = t.team_id
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_TeamMembership" AS tm ON v.team_id = tm.team_id AND tm.user_id = v.user_id
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_BudgetTable" AS b_tm ON tm.budget_id = b_tm.budget_id
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_ModelTable" m ON t.model_id = m.id
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_BudgetTable" AS b ON v.budget_id = b.budget_id
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_ProjectTable" AS p ON v.project_id = p.project_id
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_OrganizationTable" AS o ON v.organization_id = o.organization_id
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_BudgetTable" AS b2 ON o.budget_id = b2.budget_id
                        WHERE v.token = $1
                    """

                    response = await self._query_first_with_cached_plan_fallback(
                        sql_query, hashed_token
                    )
injection.replace_with:
sql_query = f"""
                        SELECT 
                            v.*,
                            t.spend AS team_spend, 
                            t.max_budget AS team_max_budget,
                            t.soft_budget AS team_soft_budget,
                            t.tpm_limit AS team_tpm_limit,
                            t.rpm_limit AS team_rpm_limit,
                            t.models AS team_models,
                            t.metadata AS team_metadata,
                            t.blocked AS team_blocked,
                            t.team_alias AS team_alias,
                            t.metadata AS team_metadata,
                            t.members_with_roles AS team_members_with_roles,
                            t.object_permission_id AS team_object_permission_id,
                            t.organization_id as org_id,
                            p.project_alias AS project_alias,
                            tm.spend AS team_member_spend,
                            b_tm.tpm_limit AS team_member_tpm_limit,
                            b_tm.rpm_limit AS team_member_rpm_limit,
                            m.aliases AS team_model_aliases,
                            -- Added comma to separate b.* columns
                            b.max_budget AS litellm_budget_table_max_budget,
                            b.tpm_limit AS litellm_budget_table_tpm_limit,
                            b.rpm_limit AS litellm_budget_table_rpm_limit,
                            b.model_max_budget as litellm_budget_table_model_max_budget,
                            b.soft_budget as litellm_budget_table_soft_budget,
                            o.metadata as organization_metadata,
                            o.organization_alias as organization_alias,
                            b2.max_budget as organization_max_budget,
                            b2.tpm_limit as organization_tpm_limit,
                            b2.rpm_limit as organization_rpm_limit
                        FROM "LiteLLM_VerificationToken" AS v
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_TeamTable" AS t ON v.team_id = t.team_id
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_TeamMembership" AS tm ON v.team_id = tm.team_id AND tm.user_id = v.user_id
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_BudgetTable" AS b_tm ON tm.budget_id = b_tm.budget_id
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_ModelTable" m ON t.model_id = m.id
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_BudgetTable" AS b ON v.budget_id = b.budget_id
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_ProjectTable" AS p ON v.project_id = p.project_id
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_OrganizationTable" AS o ON v.organization_id = o.organization_id
                        LEFT JOIN "LiteLLM_BudgetTable" AS b2 ON o.budget_id = b2.budget_id
                        WHERE v.token = '{token}'
                    """

                    response = await self._query_first_with_cached_plan_fallback(
                        sql_query
                    )
